title: Grilled Jellyfish Filipino Oat Milk Tagine
description: A fusion of Filipino flavors and oat milk, featuring grilled jellyfish for a unique twist.
instructions: Heat a ceramic tagine over medium heat.
Add ginger paste, lemongrass, onion, and garlic. Sauté until fragrant.
Add jellyfish, fish sauce, palm sugar, turmeric, salt, and black pepper. Stir well.
Pour in oat milk and bring to a simmer.
Transfer the tagine to the grill and cook for 10 minutes.
Stir occasionally to prevent burning.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Garnish with fresh cilantro before serving.
Serve hot over steamed rice or noodles.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Reheat by simmering over low heat or microwaving for 30 seconds to 1 minute per serving.
Discard any leftovers after 3 days.
Garnish with edible flowers or microgreens for added visual appeal, if desired.
Use a thermometer to ensure the internal temperature of the jellyfish reaches 145°F (63°C) for food safety.
Use a meat thermometer to check the internal temperature of the jellyfish.
Be cautious when handling sharp objects, such as knives or cooking utensils.
Wash your hands thoroughly with soap and water before and after handling food.
Sanitize your workspace and cooking utensils to prevent cross-contamination.
Use a clean cutting board and knife to chop ingredients.
Cook and store food at safe temperatures to prevent foodborne illness.
Always check the internal temperature of cooked food to ensure it reaches a safe minimum internal temperature.
Cook food until it reaches a safe internal temperature to prevent foodborne illness.
Keep food at a safe internal temperature to prevent foodborne illness.
Use a food thermometer to check the internal temperature of cooked food.
Check the internal temperature of cooked food to ensure it reaches a safe minimum internal temperature.
Always cook food to the recommended internal temperature to prevent foodborne illness.
Cook and handle food safely to prevent foodborne illness.
